reviews.review_msg: His exam was tough ngl, but he is a great prof

Honestly if you're a business student I would not recommend you take this class. As someone who did really well in Econ 105 and 103, this class was very difficult. The TA that I had did not know how to teach so tutorials felt like a waste of time but the only way you get participation marks is if you talk not just show up. Definitely take BUS 207.

Honestly one of the best professors I had the honour of studying with. Made the notoriously difficult course quite interesting and relatively easy to learn. Does not bore you with ALL the material, rather, clearly and concisely explains the material, then clarifies on specifics and various shortcuts. Very helpful with office hours and assignments.

The class is really hard in nature, but Josh helped make it easier to understand. The lectures are a bit confusing at first but going to his office hours really helps. The assignments are really hard but he basically will tell you what to do if you ask. Test averages were really low and pretty difficult.

Probably one of the best teachers I've had in University. If you love Economics, he will definitely help you deepen that. He made a class with a low pass rate very enjoyable, just overall a great teacher and great person. If you are able to take a class with him, count yourself lucky
Good guy but good luck with the exams !

Josh made microeconomics very accessible and at times entertaining. He doesn't require a textbook, and his notes are ridiculously detailed (too much even). Take solid notes and you should be fine. A warning however, his exams are tough, and you won't do well if you're the "walk into lecture half an hour late in pyjama pants" type.

I found Josh's assignments struck a great balance of challenge and concept testing, and tutorials were engaging, but exams felt comparatively much more difficult. Josh is super accessible outside of class time and is always willing to assist students who are engaging with the material. I would gladly take another course in economics with him.

If you are like me and taking this course is not a requirement, I don't recommend taking it. The problem is the weighting in this class is awful. That combined with him being a hard grader, the exams being difficult and a big part of your mark makes it hard to do well. My classes average was in the 50's for both the midterm and final.

Josh is alright, relatively nice guy who was open to questions. however the structure of ECON 201 was ridiculous. His lecture examples, tutorial questions and quizzes could not even come close to prepare you for his exams even though they are open book the averages are 15/50 because he underprepared you by sharing easy examples and then rails you

Where do I even begin. He's teaching style isn't the best. He'll go over the general concepts in class then he'll give you 'tutorial questions' which weren't explained on his lecture slides. The class average for the final was 15/50! DON"T TAKE HIS CLASS!
Very nice professor. Midterm and final was open book, open laptop. Course was pretty straight forward and no real trick questions. Overall a pretty solid experience.

as long as you follow the lecture and take notes, you'll be fine with half of the midterms and final. he shares practice exams and answers, and it really helps. the exams are open book which is both good and bad. if you understand the content and do the practices you should be fine. i personally did not study enough and just don't like econ
he's whatever. There is way to much material to remember, exams are open book and laptop but still tough as the average was in the 50s.

Josh is a kind and caring professor. His lecture slides are a condensed version of the textbook, so it's important to take good notes when he speaks to make sure you don't miss any information. His tutorial material is very helpful and I like the amount of practice exams he provides. His exams are open book, but there are trick questions.

Don't take econ 115, do yourself a favor and take 103 for extra credit. Exams are insanely difficult with low averages (52%) considering it's open book and laptop. Exams are weirdly worded, and don't expect to boost your grade with quizzes, they're hard too. Econ overall requires time to understand theories, you get lost w/ Josh's lectures.
Josh is down to earth. This characteristic helps him teach better and makes the class more enjoyable. One of the best profs to date.

Lectures were fine, but as a person who doesn't care about Econ, it did not help. All exams were open laptop, which was a good and bad thing. Good thing is that I did not need to deeply memorize the gritty details and they were readily available. Bad thing is that the exam results was very low (52% median score for recent finals).
The weighting is terrible

Josh gives a lot of practice material for all his exams and is extremely responsive to emails and great at explaining concepts if you go to his office hours. His exams are tricky and sometimes contains trick questions, and you would need to study for them just as much you would study for a closed book exam, relying on open book won't get you an A.

You don't need the textbook. Take good notes. 2 mid-terms and a Final. All open book (doesn't help much). 

Understand the concepts, attend lectures and tutorials and you'll do fine. Make sure you study, don't rely on your notes.
If you do better on the Final he will cross off a mid term so that is a big plus.

Professor himself is a great dude.

Would give this man a 10 if i could! probably one of the most caring profs at SFU. very thorough and it is very clear he enjoys teaching students. I love how he asks for feedback about his teaching in his survey because most profs would not care. His exams are very straight forward but some questions have you thinking out of the box. Good man.

Josh is a chill professor and really enthusiastic about the material. Exams were open book but pretty difficult, so make sure to study for them like you would for a normal exam. Sometimes he throws in a few trick questions as well. He gave lots of study questions and posted the answer key after the exam.

Josh was a great instructor who clearly wanted everyone to pass and offered plenty of opportunities to boost your grade. Lectures were easy to understand for someone who isn't usually number-savvy. Exams were tricky but nothing crazy as long as you made an effort to engage with the material. Josh is easy to talk to and was always happy to help.

Professor Boitnott was one of the best professors I have had. His lectures were super engaging and makes the content interesting. He was really enthusiastic and cared about his students. Exams were open book, so they were not too difficult. Gave lots of study questions and posted the answer key after the exam. Would 100% recommend courses with him!

Really nice and understanding prof; no question is stupid to him. His exams are open book and not the easiest, but as long as you go to all of the tutorials you should be able to recognize the questions and what he is looking for.

josh is a great prof and one of my favourites. he's very funny and makes lectures interesting with interactive games sometimes. every exam was open book too and there were weekly quizzes which weren't bad. he did surveys for partic. marks to see how his teaching was which i loved. accessible out of class too. didn't even use  textbook once though
Such an amazing professor!! My all time favourite prof,  he makes Econ interesting, and engaging (he's also hilarious)

The midterms are not too bad if you study well and practice many questions. The final is substantially harder and the curve can easily mess you up. The practice midterms/finals do not help at all and seem wayyy too easy compared to the actual midterm/final. If you want to secure a high grade, you have to either study ALOT or take another prof
good
The most caring professor I've ever had. Material for this class is challenging, but Josh is very approachable and answers questions clearly. 100% recommend Josh
Great prof really cares about students. material is tough but its a 4th year course

Josh was very knowledgeable and passionate about the content. His lectures were a bit all over the place but the professor was good at explaining during office hours. The problem with the class was the unavailable TA's (all) that didn't show up to office hours and/or didn't have answers or had wrong answers. A lot of content, don't fall behind!
He is very willing to help students succeed, he is always available outside of class time. Attend lectures, do as much practice as possible, and you'll do well.

I have taken a few of Josh's classes and really enjoy his teaching style. He provides lots of examples in lectures, he provides in depth lecture slides and is always available for extra help out side of class. Very organized and keeps the atmosphere light!
Josh is a nice guy. I believe he is always there for his students.

A new professor who has just taken office. He is very kind, and all the exams are open. The exam is very difficult and time is tight. You need to understand and master all the small knowledge points and spend at least ten hours of study a week to finish his exam. I heard recommending his econ201, because although it is difficult to score, you can't

Class not that hard but also not that easy. if you haven't done econ before there is a combination of both math and writing involved in this course. Assignments were an easy pass, but the exams were difficult and timed very short. The prof however is really nice though.
Very good prof. Super nice and helpful. Genuinely wants his students to succeed. Assignments and tests were very straightforward with fair marking.

Josh really cares about how effective his teaching is and always seeks feedback from his students. Many extra credit opportunities in terms of his feedback surveys; this is unheard of coming from a STEM student. He is very patient, no question is stupid to him. Tests are fair and mostly similar to practice material.

Josh is a really good professor. He is able to make Econ 201 less scary despite the difficulty and amount of math in the course. He had us do weekly quizzes and homework for participation to boost our averages. His assignments were very similar to his exams, and he provided a lot of material to work with.

Avoid 201 with him like the plague. Get ready to be slammed with theories and concepts- you wont pass if you don't study. Focus on key concepts and build theory around those. PRACTICE. He is really helpful- but only if you put in effort yourself.
Great prof but hard marker
he is my prof this summer, so far. he is very hard marker + not very clear. I don't recommand him at all
he is good prof but hard marker
he was my TA for ECON102. he is nice but very hard marking and the exam is hard. Also, he has alot of power over in the grade
